title: Capability contract
description: Machine-checkable contract for a `capability:` block on a skill entry in skills/registry.yaml. EXTENDS the shipped registry v3-lite flat convention (provides / risk_tier / verification_required, validated by tests/test_structure.py::TestRegistryCapabilitySchema) with additive data-flow fields. Every field beyond `provides`, `risk_tier`, `verification_required` is optional, so the 120+ skills without a full contract stay valid and adoption is evolutionary, not big-bang. Validated by scripts/check_architecture.py (stdlib + PyYAML, no jsonschema dependency) against a subset of JSON Schema: type, required, enum, items, properties, minItems.

provides.description: Capability tokens this skill yields (dotted lower_snake, e.g. 'hypothesis.candidates'). Non-empty.

risk_tier.description: Autonomy risk tier from .claude/rules/autonomy-budget.md. Not invented here.

verification_required.description: Skills or process gates that must verify this skill's output. Skill names must exist in the registry; process gates are a fixed allow-list (source_trace, safety_floor_check, at_least_one_kill_test). May be empty.

accepts.description: Capability tokens this skill consumes as input. Descriptive; used to explain a route.

produces.description: Outcome artifact tokens this skill yields (e.g. 'atomic_claims', 'kill_tests'). A workflow's completion.requires tokens must each be produced by some step.

requires.description: Capability tokens that MUST be provided by some other skill for this one to run (data-flow dependency). The `requires` graph must be acyclic. Distinct from top-level `depends_on` (load order) and from `verification_required` (back-reference to a verifier), neither of which participates in the acyclicity check.

preconditions.description: Human-readable conditions that must hold before invocation (e.g. 'estimand.md exists').

side_effects.description: Observable writes/effects (e.g. 'writes null_results/<id>.md'). Empty list means pure/read-only.

fallback.description: Skill name to fall back to if this one is unavailable, or null for none.

status.description: Contract maturity. Absent = stable. Only 'stable'/absent capabilities are subject to the orphan-provider gate.
